title: Aptitude: Trigonometry & Height and Distances - Q4

content.problemStatement: ### **Aptitude Practice Question** From the top of a tower 100m high, a person observes that the angle of elevation of the top of another tower is 60° and the angle of depression of the bottom of the tower is 30°. Then the height (in meters) of the second tower is.Let AB be the first tower and CD be the second tower. From figure, AB = CE Tan(30) = CE/BE 1/√3 = 100/AC ---eq1 Tan(60) = DE/BE BE = AC √3 = DE-100/AC----eq2 AC = DC-100/√3---eq3 putting eq3 in eq1 1/√3 = 100*√3/DC-100 DC-100 = 100√3*√3 DC = 400m
